root_causes.cause: An inbound access-list applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos from R1 and so R2 shows no neighbor while R1 sees R2.
evidence: get_running_config (R2) - interface Serial0/1/0: "ip access-group R2_IN in"
get_running_config (R2) - access-list R2_IN entries: sequence 10 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5; sequence 20 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6
get_ospf (R2) - Serial0/1/0: "interface_state: Point-to-Point ... neighbors: []" (no OSPF neighbors seen on R2)
get_ospf (R1) - Serial0/1/0: neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2 (state ospf-nbr-full) — R1 receives R2's OSPF packets while R2 does not see R1

root_causes.cause: An inbound access-list applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos from R1 and so R2 shows no neighbor while R1 sees R2.

data.intent_description: Investigate OSPF adjacency down on device R2 and determine required remediation to restore adjacency.

root_causes.cause: An inbound access-list applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos from R1 and so R2 shows no neighbor while R1 sees R2.
evidence: get_running_config (R2) - interface Serial0/1/0: "ip access-group R2_IN in"
get_running_config (R2) - access-list R2_IN entries: sequence 10 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5; sequence 20 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6
get_ospf (R2) - Serial0/1/0: "interface_state: Point-to-Point ... neighbors: []" (no OSPF neighbors seen on R2)
get_ospf (R1) - Serial0/1/0: neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2 (state ospf-nbr-full) — R1 receives R2's OSPF packets while R2 does not see R1

data.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN on Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6). Restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to be received on R2.

plan.problem: Inbound access-list R2_IN applied to interface Serial0/1/0 on R2 contains deny entries for destination 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6, blocking OSPF hellos and preventing OSPF adjacency with R1.
plan.fix_summary: Remove the deny entries for 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6 from access-list R2_IN (applied inbound on Serial0/1/0) so R2 can receive OSPF multicast packets and form adjacency with R1.

parameters: remove access-list R2_IN sequence 10: deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5
remove access-list R2_IN sequence 20: deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6

parsed.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN on Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6). Restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to be received on R2.

remove access-list R2_IN sequence 20: deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6

data.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN on Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6). Restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to be received on R2.

root_causes.cause: An inbound access-list applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos from R1 and so R2 shows no neighbor while R1 sees R2.
evidence: get_running_config (R2) - interface Serial0/1/0: "ip access-group R2_IN in"
get_running_config (R2) - access-list R2_IN entries: sequence 10 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5; sequence 20 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6
get_ospf (R2) - Serial0/1/0: "interface_state: Point-to-Point ... neighbors: []" (no OSPF neighbors seen on R2)
get_ospf (R1) - Serial0/1/0: neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2 (state ospf-nbr-full) — R1 receives R2's OSPF packets while R2 does not see R1

plan.problem: Inbound access-list R2_IN applied to interface Serial0/1/0 on R2 contains deny entries for destination 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6, blocking OSPF hellos and preventing OSPF adjacency with R1.
plan.fix_summary: Remove the deny entries for 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6 from access-list R2_IN (applied inbound on Serial0/1/0) so R2 can receive OSPF multicast packets and form adjacency with R1.

parameters: remove access-list R2_IN sequence 10: deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5
remove access-list R2_IN sequence 20: deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6

evidence: get_ospf (router2) shows Serial0/1/0 neighbor: neighbor_id 1.1.1.1, neighbor_address 10.0.0.1, neighbor_state ospf-nbr-full (verification tool_call_id: call_pMedWS6a6jonGhQrjHuQ4ZDC).

get_running_config (router2) shows Serial0/1/0 configured with ip 10.0.0.2 and an access-group applied only outbound (R2_TO_R1); there is no ip access-group R2_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 (verification tool_call_id: call_GVfHIrVuaV3qeHKh63QmyaPk).
get_interface_status (router2 Serial0/1/0) shows admin_state up, oper_state up, input_errors 0, output_errors 0 (verification tool_call_id: call_VNvOf2rEggJYrgPNuIpKeMdY).
remaining_issues: Access-list R2_IN still exists on R2 and contains deny entries for destination 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6 (from running-config). If R2_IN is reapplied inbound anywhere in future, OSPF multicast could be blocked again; consider removing or updating these deny ACEs or documenting intended use of R2_IN.

messages.content:
What was observed:
- R2 had no OSPF neighbor on Serial0/1/0 while R1 showed R2 as a full neighbor.

Diagnosis:
- Inbound ACL R2_IN on R2 Serial0/1/0 contained deny ACEs for 224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6, blocking OSPF hellos and preventing adjacency.

Changes made:
- Detached the R2_IN access-group from Serial0/1/0 (removed the inbound ACL).

Verification:
- PASSED. R2 now shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 on Serial0/1/0 (OSPF state FULL). Serial0/1/0 is up with no errors and there is no ip access-group R2_IN applied inbound.

What remains:
- The ACL object R2_IN still exists on R2 and contains the deny entries for OSPF multicast. If it is reapplied inbound elsewhere, adjacency can break again. Recommended: remove or edit those deny ACEs (or delete R2_IN) or ensure the ACL is only applied where intended and document the change.
